title: The Need for More Data
concept: Deep networks usually need huge datasets because their many parameters can memorize narrow patterns unless they see enough varied examples, but collecting and labeling that data is slow and expensive.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What usually shows that a model is overfitting?

options.text: It looks strong on training data but weaker on new data.
It refuses to learn the training set at all.
It removes its own hidden layers.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Overfitting means the model has memorized the training set more than it has learned patterns that generalize.

cards.title: Deep nets have a huge appetite
cards.body: A deep network with many layers and many weights is like a giant machine with lots of knobs. If you only show it a small pile of examples, it can use all those knobs to memorize tiny details instead of learning the broad patterns you actually want.

cards.title: Why more data matters
cards.body: Deep learning often works best with large, varied datasets. More examples expose the network to more poses, lighting conditions, wordings, accents, backgrounds, and edge cases, which makes memorization harder and generalization easier.

cards.title: Three practice tests are not enough
cards.body: Imagine preparing for a giant final exam using only three old quizzes. You might memorize those exact questions, but the real exam will still surprise you. Deep networks face the same problem when their training set is too small.

cards.title: Collecting real variety is costly
cards.body: A self-driving model should handle sunshine, rain, fog, night, tunnels, and construction zones. Gathering, storing, and labeling all of those scenes takes cameras, people, time, and safety checks. That is why “just get more data” can be expensive in practice.

cards.title: Big models do not magically create missing experience
cards.body: A common myth is that if the network is powerful enough, it can figure out everything from a tiny dataset. In reality, extra model capacity often makes memorization easier unless the training data is rich enough to keep it honest.

cards.title: Small dataset vs. broad dataset
cards.body: The difference is not just quantity. It is also how much real-world variety the network gets to see.

left.text:
Few examples and repeated conditions.
The model can memorize shortcuts.
New cases feel surprising.

right.text:
Many examples across many conditions.
Useful patterns repeat in different forms.
Generalization gets easier.

exercises.prompt: Why do deep networks often need more data than simpler models?

options.text: Because many parameters can fit many patterns, including fragile ones, unless the model sees enough variety.
Because GPUs refuse to train on small datasets.
Because deep networks cannot use validation sets.
Because every deep network must predict millions of classes.

exercises.explain: Right. A powerful model needs enough varied data to separate real patterns from accidental quirks.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Collecting and labeling more training data is usually free once you have chosen a model.
exercises.explain: False. Data collection often costs money, time, equipment, storage, and human labeling effort.

exercises.sentence: With too little data, a deep network may simply ___ the training set.
exercises.explain: Memorize is correct. Small datasets make it easier for a large model to learn narrow training-set details.

exercises.prompt: Which situation best shows why getting more data can be expensive?

options.text: A medical-image team must gather scans from many hospitals and pay experts to label them.
A model uses the same already-labeled photo twice during training.
A neuron changes its activation after seeing a batch.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Real data often needs devices, permissions, collection pipelines, and expert labels.

exercises.prompt: A disease-detection network is nearly perfect on its small training set but struggles on photos from different clinics, phones, and lighting setups. Why is more diverse data such a big deal here?

options.text: Because the network has enough capacity to memorize the narrow training conditions, so it needs broader examples to learn patterns that survive new settings.
Because deep networks are designed to ignore all new examples after training starts.
Because validation data should always be copied into training data.
Because collecting data matters only for linear models, not deep ones.

exercises.explain: Exactly. A deep model can fit the original clinic conditions too specifically, so broader data is what teaches it which visual clues are truly reusable.
